one_time_description: ISC loans totaling $17.12 million related to Samuel Litigation Land Claim were forgiven during the year
economic_notes: Nation is economically dependent on ISC and FNIH funding under Treaties. COVID-19 outbreak noted as significant event in March 2020. Business enterprise segment (10035134 Manitoba Ltd.) consolidated. Samuel Thorassie Land Claim ongoing negotiations.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Audit report dated March 30, 2021 - significantly delayed, Nation did not comply with FNFTA posting deadline. Major one-time item: $17.12M in ISC loans related to Samuel Thorassie Land Claim were forgiven during the year, contributing to large surplus. Nation consolidates 5 entities including 10035134 Manitoba Ltd. (business enterprise). COVID-19 noted as significant event. Legal claim of $402,764 plus unspecified damages outstanding for tobacco tax refunds. ISC recovery amounts of $965,624 outstanding on balance sheet. CMHC accountable advance of $524,566 is a new interest-bearing construction loan. isc_funding includes ISC ($24,285,519) plus ISC land claim ($1,364,461). total_government_transfers includes ISC, ISC land claim, FNIH, Province of Manitoba, CMHC, Keewatin Tribal Council, Province of Manitoba winter road, and Treaty Land Entitlement, net of deferred revenue movement. osr calculated as other revenue only ($564,342) as rental income and KTC may be government-related; rental income ($486,338) excluded from osr as it may be band housing revenue - flagged for review.
